paper.title: ResearchRubrics: A Benchmark of Prompts and Rubrics For Evaluating Deep Research Agents
paper.verification_notes: The extraction begins with the title, authors, affiliations, immutable arXiv v1 marker, and abstract; it contains the numbered body, tables/figure text, references, and appendices through the end of the 27-page PDF. This is source verification, not a deep review.

official_dataset_release.validation: All 101 non-empty JSONL lines parsed as JSON; each observed record has prompt, sample_id, domain, conceptual_breadth, logical_nesting, exploration, and rubrics. The main-branch download was byte-compared with the immutable revision URL and matched.

acquisition_purpose: Create full local primary-source evidence for queued task review-researchrubrics-expert-rubric-authoring while preserving the task for a separate deep-review worker.
inspection_scope: PDF structure and beginning/end extraction integrity
Complete 27-file code tree at the pinned commit
README dataset pointer and selected evaluator, metric, and prompt blob identities
Dataset JSONL parse, record count, rubric count, observed domain count, and immutable-revision byte equality
not_claimed: No deep paper review was performed during source acquisition.
No human or model evaluation was rerun.
Dataset labels, expert provenance, agreement, and benchmark claims have not yet been independently validated.
The code commit and dataset revision postdate arXiv v1 and must be treated as released artifacts, not assumed to be the exact manuscript-time snapshot.
